arts.ttl: Asensio fires Villa ahead against Club Brugge
arts.sum: Marco Asensio grabs his sixth goal in eight appearances for Aston Villa with a sharp finish to put the 1982 European Cup winners 1-0 up against Club Brugge in the second leg of their Champions League last-16 tie.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa 3-0 Club Brugge (agg 6-1): Marco Asensio scores twice as Villa's Champions League dream continues
arts.sum: Villa set up a Champions League quarter-final against PSG

arts.ttl: John McGinn in 'frightening' PSG fess up before Scotland hero fires ominous warning at French heavyweights
arts.sum: The midfielder captained Villa to a last 16 victory against Club Brugge with one of the favourites for the Champions League ahead

arts.ttl: Asensio’s double breaks 10-man Brugge as Aston Villa bulldoze into last eight
arts.sum: When Unai Emery accepted the challenge of reviving Aston Villa at a time when the club was fretting about relegation to the Championship, he voiced his desire to return to European competition. It was punchy, part of a grand plan and, for supporters, a particularly exciting soundbite, but back then the prospect of a Champions League quarter-final date with Paris Saint-Germain, one of Emery’s former clubs, felt fanciful. The Villa manager has unequivocally delivered on his wish. The Champions League furniture: the oversized badges, the giant tifos and 3D signage will get at least another outing this season. “Paris away, olé olé,” a delirious Holte End sang. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Aston Villa 3-0 Club Brugge (6-1): Highlights, man of the match, stats as Marco Asensio sets up Champions League quarter-final tie
arts.sum: Sports Mole looks at the highlights, man of the match, and stats from Wednesday's Champions League clash between Aston Villa and Club Brugge.

arts.ttl: Marco Asensio double helps Villa into Champions League quarter-final against PSG
arts.sum: Asensio climbed off the bench to score a second-half double as Aston Villa won their last-16 tie with Club Brugge 6-1 on aggregate.

arts.ttl: The Aston Villa star who represents remarkable rise and offers Champions League boost
arts.sum: Aston Villa 3-0 Club Brugge (6-1 on agg.): Marco Asensio scored twice as Unai Emery’s side reached the quarter-finals, where they will face PSG

arts.ttl: John Townley's Aston Villa player ratings after 3-0 win over Club Brugge
arts.sum: Unai Emery's side started the second leg with a two-goal lead having won 3-1 at the Jan Breydel Stadium last Tuesday and were playing against 10 men for the vast majority of the game after Kyriani Sabbe was sent off in the 17th minute. Emi Martinez played an inch-perfect long ball to Rashford in behind, who was bearing down on Simon Mignolet in the Brugge goal, before Sabbe brought him down outside the box.

arts.ttl: Asensio fires Villa ahead against Club Brugge
arts.sum: Marco Asensio grabs his sixth goal in eight appearances for Aston Villa with a sharp finish to put the 1982 European Cup winners 1-0 up against Club Brugge in the second leg of their Champions League last-16 tie.

arts.ttl: Asensio scores again as Aston Villa reach quarter-finals
arts.sum: Watch highlights as Marco Asensio comes off the bench to grab his sixth and seventh goals in eight Aston Villa appearances to secure a 3-0 victory against Club Brugge and send the 1982 winners through to the quarter-finals of the Champions League.
